title: Lesson 2 — The Restaurant Opening

words.definition: a harsh, discordant mixture of sounds
words.example: The cacophony of car horns and jackhammers made the phone call impossible.

words.definition: to give a false impression of; to contradict
words.example: Her calm voice belied the panic she felt inside.

words.definition: modest or shy because of a lack of self-confidence
words.example: The diffident intern rarely spoke up, even when she knew the answer.

words.definition: fluent, persuasive, and expressive in speech or writing
words.example: His eloquent toast left half the room in tears.

words.definition: very attentive to detail and accuracy; hard to please
words.example: A fastidious editor, she flagged every misplaced comma.

words.definition: to make someone less angry or hostile; to soothe
words.example: He offered a refund to placate the furious customer.
His quixotic plan to end all traffic by banning cars amused the council.

words.definition: a person who flatters powerful people to gain advantage
words.example: The manager was surrounded by sycophants who praised his every word.

words.definition: reserved or uncommunicative in speech; saying little
words.example: The taciturn guard answered our questions with a single nod.

words.definition: to clear of blame or suspicion; to show to be justified
words.example: The new evidence vindicated the man imprisoned years earlier.

words.definition: cautious and watchful about possible danger or problems
words.example: Investors grew wary after the third quarter of losses.

words.definition: filled with energetic and often uncritical enthusiasm
words.example: The zealous volunteers knocked on every door in the district.

story: The grand opening was a [[cacophony|cacophony]] of clattering pans and shouted orders, a chaos that [[belie|belied]] the calm the owner tried to project. Chef Dupont, usually [[diffident|diffident]] and reluctant to speak, turned strangely [[eloquent|eloquent]] whenever he described his sauces. He was famously [[fastidious|fastidious]], sending back any plate a shade off-color. His business partner scrambled to [[placate|placate]] the impatient investors, several of whom thought the whole venture [[quixotic|quixotic]] — a beautiful but doomed dream. Trouble came the night a [[sycophant|sycophant]] on staff, forever fawning over the loudest backer, began spreading rumors. The head waiter, [[taciturn|taciturn]] but sharp-eyed, said nothing and missed nothing. When a glowing review finally arrived, it seemed to [[vindicate|vindicate]] every stubborn choice Dupont had made. The investors, once so [[wary|wary]] of him, grew [[zealous|zealous]] overnight, toasting the very chef they had nearly abandoned.
